title: Survivor Governance Risk Index (SGRI): Conceptual and Methodological White Book
subtitle: Version 0.1 Foundational Release
title_normalized: survivor governance risk index sgri conceptual and methodological white book

creators.name: Wu, Shao-Yuan
creators.affiliation: Global AI Governance and Policy Research Center, EPINOVA LLC

alternate_identifiers.description: Internal EPINOVA Index White Book identifier

description: This white book introduces the Survivor Governance Risk Index (SGRI), a conceptual and methodological framework for assessing structural political risk under AI-driven automation. It defines Survivor Governance, develops a three-dimensional indicator architecture, explains composite index construction and risk bands, provides an illustrative calculation, and outlines data sources, typologies, limitations, and future empirical upgrade pathways.
abstract: The Survivor Governance Risk Index (SGRI) White Book develops a structural early-warning framework for assessing whether AI-driven automation may erode the socio-economic population base required for inclusive and effective political participation. Rather than measuring democratic quality, regime type, or national AI capability, SGRI evaluates whether political influence is becoming concentrated among structurally non-displaceable groups while automation-exposed populations retain formal rights but lose effective political agency. The framework operationalizes this risk through three dimensions: Material Survivorship, Political Participation Skew, and Corrective Capacity. It defines nine core indicators, specifies normalization and aggregation procedures, proposes a composite formula, introduces qualitative risk bands, and presents ideal-type risk configurations for comparative analysis. The white book emphasizes profile-based diagnosis rather than country ranking and positions SGRI as a modular risk layer for future global AI governance, digital transformation, sustainability, and competitiveness index systems.

dates.description: Initial public release of SGRI White Book v0.1.

imprint.title: Survivor Governance Risk Index (SGRI): Conceptual and Methodological White Book

tables.title: Core Dimensions and Indicator Composition of SGRI
tables.description: Defines the three primary SGRI dimensions—Material Survivorship, Political Participation Skew, and Corrective Capacity—and their nine core indicators.

tables.title: SGRI Risk Bands and Interpretive Framework
tables.description: Summarizes qualitative risk bands for interpreting SGRI as an anticipatory structural governance-risk signal rather than an ordinal ranking or performance grade.

tables.title: NAES Levels and Risk Interpretation
tables.description: Classifies Non-Automatable Employment Share levels and their implications for survivorship concentration risk.

tables.title: ICS Levels and Risk Interpretation
tables.description: Classifies Income Concentration among Survivors and its relationship to survivorship-based economic dominance.

tables.title: RPAD Levels and Risk Interpretation
tables.description: Classifies Re-entry Probability after AI Displacement and its implications for displacement reversibility and structural exclusion.

tables.title: EPC Level and Risk Interpretation
tables.description: Classifies Employment–Participation Correlation and its implications for political participation dependence on employment stability.
tables.source: Author’s SGRI Political Participation Skew methodology

tables.title: SORI Level and Risk Interpretation
tables.description: Classifies Survivor Over-Representation Index levels and their implications for institutional over-representation of structurally non-displaceable groups.
tables.source: Author’s SGRI Political Participation Skew methodology

tables.title: PRA Level and Risk Interpretation
tables.description: Classifies Policy Responsiveness Asymmetry and its implications for survivor-favoring policy skew.
tables.source: Author’s SGRI Political Participation Skew methodology

tables.title: EMP Level and Interpretation
tables.description: Classifies Economic Membership Preservation as a corrective-capacity indicator that buffers AI-driven displacement risk.

tables.title: PEO Level and Interpretation
tables.description: Classifies Political Entry Openness as a corrective-capacity indicator of access to meaningful political participation channels.

tables.title: DBA Level and Interpretation
tables.description: Classifies Democratic Binding of AI Decisions as a corrective-capacity indicator of oversight, auditability, and redress for AI-mediated decisions.

tables.title: Hypothetical Input Data and Risk Band Assignment
tables.description: Provides a conceptual example of normalized M, P, and C indicators, dimension aggregation, composite SGRI calculation, and moderate-risk classification.
